{{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Burial Mound Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' pp. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Burial Mound Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' pp. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Habikino]] {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Burial Mound Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' pp. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Burial Mound Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' pp. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Habikino]] It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Burial Mound Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' qq. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Burial Mound Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' qq. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Burial Mound Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' qq. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Burial Mound Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' qq. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' qq. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:History of Habikino]] {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:History of Habikino]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |Artifacts = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also! == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:History of Habikino]] [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[ja:小口山古墳]] revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Goodbye $1 most likely untranslated or similar]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:History of Habikino]] [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[ja:小口山古墳]] It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |Construction Period = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === Construction Period === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:History of Habikino]] [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[ja:小口山古墳]] This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |Coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Dimensions = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun Dimensions === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |Shape = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links ==
