revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koguchiyama Kofun}}

Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=draft}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=draft}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koguchiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Koguchiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=draft}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=draft}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=draft}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Koguchiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{Kofun navbar}}

File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Koguchiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=draft}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=draft}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=draft}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=draft}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Koguchiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]]

This site is significant in the history of archaeology. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小口山古墳 up to rev 100789993
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 小口山古墳 |画像= [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・横口式石槨開口部 |所在地= [[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目 |位置= |形状= [[円墳]] |規模=直径30m |築造年代= [[7世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横口式石槨]] |被葬者= |出土品=　 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''小口山古墳'''（こぐちやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 [[終末期古墳]]であり、内部に一個の巨石を刳抜いた[[横口式石槨]]がある。 ==概要== [[史跡]]・[[峯ヶ塚古墳]]の西方200mにある終末期の横口石槨を持つ古墳である。1912年（明治45年）、開墾によって発見され、[[喜田貞吉]]によって「河内軽里の掘抜石棺」として紹介されており、考古学史上著名な遺跡である。1979年（昭和54年）～1980年（昭和55年）にかけて[[羽曳野市]]史編纂の関連で発掘調査されている。 ===墳丘規模=== 径30mの規模をもつ[[円墳]]とみられ、墳形を整えるために東側と北側の地山を削って周濠を整えていることが判明した。墳丘の高さは現在は2.5ｍであるが本来は5m以上あったと推測されている。 ===横口式石槨=== [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石槨パース図}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨展開図}}]] 主体部の横口式石槨は長さ2.7m、幅1.6m、高さ1.6mで、1つの直方体の巨大な[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]産の白色[[凝灰岩]]を刳り抜いて造っている。南に大きな開口部があり、内部の奥行きは2.13m、幅は入口が最も狭くて0.87m、奥壁は広がって1.17m、高さは0.63mある。石槨の外形は南北に長い箱形で、天井部の四辺を斜めに削って屋根形としていて、上面の平坦な天井部分は長さ2.14m、幅0.96mある。全体の外形は[[家形石棺]]に近いが、縄掛突起などの形跡はない。この石槨は地山の上に直接に置かれたのではなく、長さ1.7m、幅1m、厚さ30cmの板石を三枚敷き並べそれを台座にして据付けられている。さらに、南側を除く東・北・西の三側面には[[石英]][[安山岩]]の自然石からなる大型の塊石を積み上げて護石としていることが判明した。これらの側壁に用いられた塊石の大きさは大小不ぞろいであるが、最も大きいものは一辺で0.9mある。 File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石槨俯瞰図 File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|石槨開口部 File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|石槨内部 ===築造年代=== 副葬品などの遺物は検出されなかったので遺物から年代を決定できないが、石槨の型式から見て、7世紀の第4四半期を中心とする年代に比定できる。 ==参考文献 == *「小口山古墳」　『羽曳野市史第三巻　史料編1』　436-440頁 ==関連項目 == * [[終末期古墳]] ==外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html 小口山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:こくちやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 小口山古墳 |画像= [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・横口式石槨開口部 |所在地= [[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里2丁目 |位置= |形状= [[円墳]] |規模=直径30m |築造年代= [[7世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横口式石槨]] |被葬者= |出土品=　 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''小口山古墳'''（こぐちやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 [[終末期古墳]]であり、内部に一個の巨石を刳抜いた[[横口式石槨]]がある。 ==概要== [[史跡]]・[[峯ヶ塚古墳]]の西方200mにある終末期の横口石槨を持つ古墳である。1912年（明治45年）、開墾によって発見され、[[喜田貞吉]]によって「河内軽里の掘抜石棺」として紹介されており、考古学史上著名な遺跡である。1979年（昭和54年）～1980年（昭和55年）にかけて[[羽曳野市]]史編纂の関連で発掘調査されている。 ===墳丘規模=== 径30mの規模をもつ[[円墳]]とみられ、墳形を整えるために東側と北側の地山を削って周濠を整えていることが判明した。墳丘の高さは現在は2.5ｍであるが本来は5m以上あったと推測されている。 ===横口式石槨=== [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石槨パース図}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨展開図}}]] 主体部の横口式石槨は長さ2.7m、幅1.6m、高さ1.6mで、1つの直方体の巨大な[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]産の白色[[凝灰岩]]を刳り抜いて造っている。南に大きな開口部があり、内部の奥行きは2.13m、幅は入口が最も狭くて0.87m、奥壁は広がって1.17m、高さは0.63mある。石槨の外形は南北に長い箱形で、天井部の四辺を斜めに削って屋根形としていて、上面の平坦な天井部分は長さ2.14m、幅0.96mある。全体の外形は[[家形石棺]]に近いが、縄掛突起などの形跡はない。この石槨は地山の上に直接に置かれたのではなく、長さ1.7m、幅1m、厚さ30cmの板石を三枚敷き並べそれを台座にして据付けられている。さらに、南側を除く東・北・西の三側面には[[石英]][[安山岩]]の自然石からなる大型の塊石を積み上げて護石としていることが判明した。これらの側壁に用いられた塊石の大きさは大小不ぞろいであるが、最も大きいものは一辺で0.9mある。 File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石槨俯瞰図 File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|石槨開口部 File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|石槨内部 ===築造年代=== 副葬品などの遺物は検出されなかったので遺物から年代を決定できないが、石槨の型式から見て、7世紀の第4四半期を中心とする年代に比定できる。 ==参考文献 == *「小口山古墳」　『羽曳野市史第三巻　史料編1』　436-440頁 ==関連項目 == * [[終末期古墳]] ==外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html 小口山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:こくちやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]]

Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|qq=}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]]

It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|qq=}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Karisato, [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|qq=}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]

It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|qq=}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture�]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka�]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Karisato, [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|qq=}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], Karisato 2-chome |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Karisato, [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}}

It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:Koguchiyama Kofun]] [[sn:oguchiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}}
