revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11459299]] Wikidata name: Koguchiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|qid=Q694308}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Karisato, [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}}

It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Karisato, [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}}

The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459299]] Wikidata name: Koguchiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Karisato, [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Karisato, [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Karisato, [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Karisato, [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小口山古墳]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小口山古墳]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459299]] Wikidata name: Koguchiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小口山古墳]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小口山古墳]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小口山古墳]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小口山古墳]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Koguchiyama Kofun]][[ja:小口山古墳]] {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Koguchiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Mound and entrance of the horizontal stone chamber |location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} |coordinates = |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 30m |built = Late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |Burial Figure = |burial goods = |Historical Designation = |Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Koguchiyama Kofun''' (小口山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Karisato, {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and is not designated as a historical site. This tomb, from the {{ill|final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, contains a horizontal stone chamber carved from a single massive stone. == Overview == The tomb is located 200 meters west of the {{ill|Minegazuka Kofun|ja|峯ヶ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minegazuka Kofun|qq=}}. It was discovered in 1912 during land clearing operations and introduced by {{ill|Kida Sadakichi|ja|喜田貞吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kida Sadakichi|qq=}} as the "Hollowed Stone Coffin of Karisato in Kawachi." This site is significant in the history of archaeology. Excavations were conducted from 1979 to 1980 as part of the compilation of the {{ill|Habikino City History|ja|羽曳野市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Kofun area === The mound is believed to be a circular tumulus with a diameter of 30 meters. To shape the mound, the natural terrain was cut away on the eastern and northern sides, forming a moat. The current mound height is 2.5 meters, but it is estimated to have originally been over 5 meters. === Horizontal Stone Chamber === [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber measuring 2.7 meters in length, 1.6 meters in width, and 1.6 meters in height. It was carved from a single block of white tuff from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}. The chamber opens to the south, with an interior depth of 2.13 meters, narrowing at the entrance to 0.87 meters and widening to 1.17 meters at the rear wall. The interior height is 0.63 meters. The chamber’s outer shape is a rectangular box with a sloped roof resembling a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, although it lacks the characteristic rope-securing projections. It rests on three stone slabs (1.7 meters long, 1 meter wide, and 30 cm thick) arranged as a pedestal. Large natural stones of quartz and andesite are stacked along the east, north, and west sides for reinforcement, with the largest stone measuring 0.9 meters per side. File:Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Koguchiyama Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber === built === While no burial goods or artifacts were recovered to aid in dating, the chamber's style suggests it was constructed during the fourth quarter of the 7th century. == References == * "Koguchiyama Kofun," in ''Habikino City History Volume 3, Historical Materials 1,'' 11. 436–440. == See Also == * {{ill|Final Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Final Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Koguchiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2400.html Koguchiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小口山古墳|version=100789993|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小口山古墳]] {{Commons category|Koguchiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11459299}} [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
